evals.name: sqlalchemy-full-refactoring
evals.prompt: Use the yolo-api-data-layer skill to refactor the YOLO FastAPI service from raw sqlite3 to SQLAlchemy ORM. Create db.py with engine and session factory, models.py with ORM classes, update app.py to use dependency injection for all database operations, and refactor all tests to use file-based SQLite fixtures.
evals.expected_output: Complete SQLAlchemy refactoring of YOLO API with db.py module, models.py module, updated app.py using ORM for all database operations, dependency injection in all endpoints, file-based test fixtures, and ORM patterns throughout. All existing API contracts preserved. No raw SQL or sqlite3 module usage remaining.
assertions: services/yolo/db.py created with SQLAlchemy engine, SessionLocal, Base, and get_db()
services/yolo/models.py created with PredictionSession and DetectionObject ORM models
services/yolo/app.py imports SQLAlchemy modules and models, removes sqlite3
All endpoints accept db: Session = Depends(get_db) parameter
All database operations use ORM: db.query(), db.add(), db.commit()
No raw SQL strings anywhere in app.py
No row['column'] dict access - uses obj.column attribute access
save_prediction_session and save_detection_object helpers accept db: Session
Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine) replaces init_db() SQL
services/yolo/tests/test_api.py has test_engine fixture using tempfile.mkstemp()
test_engine creates file-based SQLite database (not :memory:)
setup_db fixture has autouse=True and depends on test_engine
db_session fixture depends on test_engine
All test operations use ORM patterns
No sqlite3 imports in tests
All original endpoints unchanged: /predict, /prediction/{uid}, /predictions/label/{label}, /predictions/score/{min_score}
All status codes preserved: 200, 400, 404, 503
YoloPredictResponse schema unchanged
CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD environment variable handling preserved
YOLO model inference logic unchanged

evals.name: add-orm-endpoint-recent-sessions
evals.prompt: Add a new GET /predictions/recent endpoint to the YOLO API that returns the N most recent prediction sessions with their detection counts and labels, using SQLAlchemy ORM queries ordered by timestamp.
evals.expected_output: New GET /predictions/recent endpoint that accepts optional limit parameter, queries PredictionSession table ordered by timestamp descending using ORM, loads related detection_objects via relationship, and returns JSON with session metadata and detection summaries.
assertions: Endpoint GET /predictions/recent is added to app.py
Endpoint signature includes db: Session = Depends(get_db) parameter
Endpoint signature includes optional limit parameter (default 10)
Uses db.query(PredictionSession).order_by(desc(PredictionSession.timestamp))
Uses .limit(limit).all() to fetch limited results
Response includes uid, timestamp, detection_count, labels fields
Accesses s.detection_objects relationship instead of manual JOIN
Returns list of dicts with correct structure
No raw SQL used
Endpoint returns 200 status with correct JSON structure
Existing endpoints remain unchanged

evals.name: add-database-table-user-feedback
evals.prompt: Add a UserFeedback table to the YOLO API database to store user ratings and comments per prediction. Define ORM model with foreign key to PredictionSession, create endpoint POST /predictions/{uid}/feedback to accept feedback, and add ORM relationship with cascade configuration.
evals.expected_output: New UserFeedback ORM model in models.py with rating and comment fields, foreign key to PredictionSession, relationship with backref for reverse access, new POST /predictions/{uid}/feedback endpoint accepting rating and optional comment, proper validation and error handling.
assertions: UserFeedback class defined in models.py
UserFeedback has __tablename__ = 'user_feedback'
UserFeedback has id (Integer, primary_key, autoincrement)
UserFeedback has prediction_uid (String, ForeignKey to prediction_sessions.uid, indexed)
UserFeedback has rating (Integer) for ratings
UserFeedback has comment (String, nullable=True)
UserFeedback has created_at (DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
UserFeedback defines relationship to PredictionSession with backref='feedback'
POST /predictions/{uid}/feedback endpoint added
Endpoint includes db: Session = Depends(get_db)
Endpoint accepts rating (required) and comment (optional) parameters
Endpoint validates that prediction exists before saving feedback
Endpoint returns 404 if prediction not found
Endpoint creates UserFeedback ORM instance and saves with db.add()/db.commit()
Endpoint returns 200 with confirmation message
Base.metadata.create_all() automatically creates user_feedback table
New table does not affect existing endpoints

evals.name: add-column-processing-time-ms
evals.prompt: Add a processing_time_ms column to the PredictionSession table to track inference time, update the POST /predict endpoint to capture and store this metric, and update save_prediction_session helper to accept and persist the processing time.
evals.expected_output: New processing_time_ms column added to PredictionSession model as Integer column, POST /predict endpoint calculates elapsed time in milliseconds, save_prediction_session helper accepts processing_time_ms parameter, column populated for all new predictions, existing predictions nullable for backward compatibility.
assertions: PredictionSession model in models.py has new processing_time_ms column
processing_time_ms is Integer column type
processing_time_ms column has nullable=True for backward compatibility
save_prediction_session helper signature includes processing_time_ms parameter
save_prediction_session creates PredictionSession with processing_time_ms value
POST /predict endpoint captures start_time = time.time() at beginning
POST /predict calculates processing_time = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)
POST /predict passes processing_time_ms to save_prediction_session()
YoloPredictResponse may optionally include processing_time_ms field
Column added to existing PredictionSession without breaking existing queries
time module imported in app.py
Base.metadata.create_all() creates column for new databases
Existing endpoints continue to work

evals.name: configure-database-backend-postgres
evals.prompt: Configure the YOLO API database layer to support both SQLite (development) and PostgreSQL (production) via environment variables DB_BACKEND, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME. Update db.py to conditionally construct connection strings.
evals.expected_output: db.py reads environment variables (DB_BACKEND, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME) with sensible defaults, constructs appropriate SQLAlchemy database URL for SQLite or PostgreSQL, engine creation works identically for both backends, no code changes needed to switch databases.
assertions: db.py defines DB_BACKEND = os.getenv('DB_BACKEND', 'sqlite')
db.py defines DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME with defaults
db.py conditionally builds DATABASE_URL based on DB_BACKEND
SQLite URL format: 'sqlite:///./predictions.db'
PostgreSQL URL format: 'postgresql://{DB_USER}:{DB_PASSWORD}@{DB_HOST}:{DB_PORT}/{DB_NAME}'
create_engine() uses appropriate connect_args per backend
SQLite includes connect_args={'check_same_thread': False}
PostgreSQL omits SQLite-specific connect_args
engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, connect_args=...)
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine) unchanged regardless of backend
README documents environment variables needed for PostgreSQL
Local development uses default SQLite without setting environment variables
Production can switch to PostgreSQL by setting 5 environment variables
No hardcoded database URLs in code
All existing ORM models and queries work with both backends

evals.name: implement-cascade-delete-session
evals.prompt: Implement cascade delete pattern in the YOLO API database so that deleting a PredictionSession automatically deletes all its related DetectionObject records. Add relationship with cascade configuration and create DELETE /predictions/{uid} endpoint.
evals.expected_output: PredictionSession model defines DetectionObject relationship with cascade='all, delete-orphan' configuration. New DELETE /predictions/{uid} endpoint queries session by uid, deletes it (cascading deletes all detection objects), returns 200 on success or 404 if not found.
assertions: PredictionSession.detection_objects relationship defined with cascade='all, delete-orphan'
relationship uses back_populates='prediction_session' for bidirectional reference
DELETE /predictions/{uid} endpoint added to app.py
Endpoint includes db: Session = Depends(get_db)
Endpoint queries PredictionSession by uid
Endpoint returns 404 if prediction not found
Endpoint uses db.delete(session) and db.commit()
Single db.delete() call cascades to delete all detection_objects
No manual deletion of detection_objects required
Endpoint returns 200 with confirmation: {'status': 'deleted', 'uid': uid}
Cascade delete is atomic: entire operation succeeds or rolls back
Related detection_objects are automatically removed from database
Cascade delete works for child tables with foreign key relationships

evals.name: test-fixtures-file-based-database
evals.prompt: Implement test fixtures for the YOLO API that use file-based SQLite database (not in-memory) to enable proper TestClient thread sharing. Create test_engine, setup_db, and db_session fixtures that share a single database file across all threads.
evals.expected_output: test_engine fixture creates temporary file-based SQLite database using tempfile.mkstemp(), creates all tables, and yields engine. setup_db (autouse) overrides get_db dependency with test session factory from same engine. db_session fixture provides session for manual database operations in tests. All fixtures depend on test_engine to ensure single shared database.
assertions: test_engine fixture has scope='function'
test_engine uses tempfile.mkstemp() to create temp database file
test_engine creates engine with f'sqlite:///{db_path}' (file-based, not :memory:)
test_engine includes connect_args={'check_same_thread': False}
test_engine calls Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)
test_engine yields engine for use by other fixtures
test_engine cleanup: Base.metadata.drop_all(bind=engine), engine.dispose(), os.unlink(db_path)
setup_db fixture has autouse=True
setup_db depends on test_engine parameter
setup_db creates TestingSessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=test_engine)
setup_db overrides app.dependency_overrides[get_db] with test session factory
setup_db yields for test execution
setup_db clears app.dependency_overrides.clear() after test
db_session fixture depends on test_engine parameter
db_session creates session from same engine: SessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=test_engine)
db_session yields fresh session for test
db_session closes session in finally block
TestClient uses app with injected database via dependency_overrides
File-based database is thread-safe and accessible from TestClient background thread
No :memory: databases used in tests
